10D1:0330.label: sb_lazy_init_guard
10D1:0330.comment:
One-shot initialisation guard for the SB/MIDI subsystem.
Checks [0x0134] and [0x0135] init flags; if both are zero, runs the
first-time MIDI device setup sequence: calls func_0AB2 with function codes
0x42 (MIDI init, params 3/1), 0x70, and 0xB0, then sets [0x0135]=1 to
prevent re-entry.

10D1:036A.comment:
SB/MIDI initialisation with mode selection.
Calls func_014C to validate the mode argument, then runs the lazy init
guard, then scans IVT entries 0x80–0xBF for a loaded SB16 TSR
(via sb_find_tsr_segment).  If found, the TSR segment is passed as the
mode to func_060C; if not found, 0 is passed (no-hardware fallback).

10D1:06B4.label: sb_find_tsr_segment

10D1:060C.label: sb_init_with_mode
10D1:060C.comment:
Initialises the SB/MIDI subsystem with a given mode.
Arg [bp+04]: mode (0 = no SB hardware found; non-zero = TSR segment).
Calls the lazy init guard, then dispatches through func_0AB2 with
function code 0x04E2 and the mode value.

0019:440F.label: sbmidi_apply_config
0019:440F.comment:
Applies the SBMIDI hardware configuration table for the active device.
Calls sbmidi_config_table_read only when [0x034A]==0 (first call).

0019:4427.label: sbmidi_config_table_read
0019:4427.comment:
Walks the packed config table at DS:0x3DDB searching for an entry matching
the current hardware type (BL) and version (BH).  On a match, unpacks
three fields into sbmidi_ready_flag [0x0323], [0x0326], and [0x0327].
Entry format at DS:[SI]: { hw_type:u8, version:u8, config_byte:u8, flag_byte:u8 }
Terminated by 0xFF.

0019:445E.label: sbmidi_lookup_cmd
0019:445E.comment:
Table-search helper.  Scans the CS-segment table at 0x3DDB for AH=command.
Input: AH=command byte, AL=version, BL=hw_type.
Output: AH=0 on match or fallback.

0019:4499.label: sbmidi_not_ready
0019:4499.comment:
SBMIDI error stub installed while the driver is not yet initialised.
Unconditionally sets sbmidi_ready_flag=0x01 (ERROR) and returns CF=1
to signal the caller that SBMIDI is unavailable.

0019:44AA.label: sbmidi_int2fh_error
0019:44AA.comment:
INT 2Fh error exit: calls sbmidi_not_ready (sets flag=1, CF=1) then far-returns.
Reached when the TSR is invoked but not yet fully initialised.

0019:44AE.label: sbmidi_int2fh_ok
0019:44AE.comment:
INT 2Fh success exit: clears CF then far-returns.
Reached when SBMIDI has accepted a function call successfully.

0019:44B0.label: sbmidi_int2fh_handler
0019:44B0.comment:
INT 2Fh multiplexer handler for SBMIDI (multiplex ID 0x11).
Chained from the previous INT 2Fh vector via 0B73:007D.
Dispatch:
  AH != 0x11 → chain to next handler (jmp far 0070:0005)
  AH == 0x11, AL == 0x00 → detection probe; return CF=0 (we are present)
  AH == 0x11, AL != 0x00 → function call; if not ready, call sbmidi_not_ready (CF=1)

lineComments.10D1:06B8: DI = 0x0202: CS field of IVT entry for INT 0x80 (scan start)
lineComments.10D1:06BD: read the CS (segment) of this IVT handler entry
lineComments.10D1:06C4: point ES at the handler segment to probe it
lineComments.10D1:06C6: check for 'SB' signature (0x53,0x42) at segment offset 0
lineComments.10D1:0395: AX=0 means no SB TSR found in IVT
lineComments.10D1:03DE: pass 0 (no SB) as mode argument to sb_init_with_mode
lineComments.10D1:0364: [0x0135]=1 marks this one-shot init as done
lineComments.0019:44A8: CF=1 tells the INT 2Fh caller that SBMIDI is unavailable
lineComments.0019:44B6: AL=0 is the standard INT 2Fh detection probe; any non-zero value is a real function call
lineComments.0019:44B8: detection probe: return without calling sbmidi_not_ready so CF stays clear (we are installed)
lineComments.0019:44BA: function call when driver not initialised → always returns CF=1 error
lineComments.0019:ABA5: probe SBMIDI: INT 2Fh AH=0x11 AL=0x23 — ask SBMIDI to prepare MIDI output
lineComments.0019:ABAA: CF=0 (jae taken) = SBMIDI accepted the call; CF=1 = not installed or not ready → 'SBMIDI not installed'

gaps.10D1:06CD: success path: reads TSR fields and returns CS in AX — never executes because no SB TSR is installed in IVT 0x80–0xBF
gaps.10D1:0399: success path when SB TSR found — never executes in this log
